project: TXGNN-StaR: Stability-Aware GNN Framework with Reliability-Quantified Toxicophore Discovery

pbes_module.description: Perturbation-Based Explanation Stability - measures consistency of GNNExplainer attributions across K=10 graph-isomorphic molecular variants via Spearman correlation

sss_component.description: Structural Sparsity Score - Gini coefficient of atom attribution distribution

tas_component.description: Toxicophore Agreement Score - overlap between top-important atoms and known structural alerts (Kazius et al. 2005, ToxAlerts-consistent SMARTS patterns)

eri_metric.formula: ERI(G) = alpha*ACS(G) + beta*SSS(G) + gamma*TAS(G), re-normalized when TAS undefined

correlation_findings.key_finding: ACS and TAS show near-zero independent correlation (r=0.013, p=0.94), indicating explanation STABILITY and CHEMICAL VALIDITY are distinct, complementary reliability dimensions - supporting the need for a multi-component ERI.

clintox_cross_validation.conclusion: No statistically significant difference between Tox21 and ClinTox ACS distributions (p=0.34), demonstrating PBES generalizes robustly across datasets.
